title: News and updates | Employment New Zealand
content:
## News and updates Ngā rongo kōrero me ngā whakahou
News and media releases from Employment New Zealand.
- 3 Jun 2026 Auckland company charged migrant worker almost $45,000 for his job An Auckland-based landscaping company that made a migrant worker pay almost $45,000 for a job in New Zealand has been ordered by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) to repay the money.
An Auckland-based landscaping company that made a migrant worker pay almost $45,000 for a job in New Zealand has been ordered by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) to repay the money.
- 26 May 2026 Kiwifruit company owner fined $40,000 for exploiting migrant workers The owner of a now-liquidated kiwifruit contracting company has been ordered to pay $40,000 in penalties by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) for exploiting migrant workers, including requiring one to pay more than $23,000 to secure his job. Migrant exploitation
The owner of a now-liquidated kiwifruit contracting company has been ordered to pay $40,000 in penalties by the Employment Relations Authority (ERA) for exploiting migrant workers, including requiring one to pay more than $23,000 to secure his job.
- Migrant exploitation
- 29 Apr 2026 Christchurch massage business fined $210,000 for exploiting migrant workers The Employment Relations Authority (ERA) has ordered a Christchurch massage business and its sole director to pay $210,000 in penalties for breaching multiple minimum employment standards involving vulnerable migrant workers. Migrant exploitation Employment agreements
The Employment Relations Authority (ERA) has ordered a Christchurch massage business and its sole director to pay $210,000 in penalties for breaching multiple minimum employment standards involving vulnerable migrant workers.
- Migrant exploitation
- Employment agreements
- 29 Apr 2026 Bay of Plenty kiwifruit orchards under MBIE spotlight Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) visited several Bay of Plenty towns last week as part of a targeted operation to ensure kiwifruit orchards are playing by the rules. Employment agreements
Teams from the Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) visited several Bay of Plenty towns last week as part of a targeted operation to ensure kiwifruit orchards are playing by the rules.
- Employment agreements
- 31 Mar 2026 Know your employment rights and obligations at Easter With Easter just around the corner, it's important for both employers and employees to understand their rights and responsibilities over this period.
With Easter just around the corner, it's important for both employers and employees to understand their rights and responsibilities over this period.
- 12 Mar 2026 Employment Leave Bill 2026 A new Employment Leave Bill has been introduced to replace the Holidays Act. Until the new Bill takes effect, employers must continue to follow the current law.
A new Employment Leave Bill has been introduced to replace the Holidays Act. Until the new Bill takes effect, employers must continue to follow the current law.
- 9 Mar 2026 Minimum wage is increasing on 1 April 2026 The minimum wage rates will increase on 1 April 2026. If you are an employer, business owner, or manager, now is a good time to make sure you are prepared for these changes. Minimum wage
The minimum wage rates will increase on 1 April 2026. If you are an employer, business owner, or manager, now is a good time to make sure you are prepared for these changes.
- 26 Feb 2026 Regulators take integrated approach to construction sector crack down 147 construction businesses visited in large-scale integrated operation. Migrant exploitation
147 construction businesses visited in large-scale integrated operation.
- Migrant exploitation
- 21 Feb 2026 Employment Relations Act changes take effect today A series of changes to the Employment Relations Act come into force today, following the passing of the Employment Relations Amendment Act 2025
A series of changes to the Employment Relations Act come into force today, following the passing of the Employment Relations Amendment Act 2025
- 11 Dec 2025 Holiday trading and work rules: What employers and employees need to know With the festive season fast approaching the Labour Inspectorate is urging employers and employees to understand their legal obligations around trading and working on public holidays. Public holidays
With the festive season fast approaching the Labour Inspectorate is urging employers and employees to understand their legal obligations around trading and working on public holidays.
